page: Amazon Web Services r3.8xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The configuration includes 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 244.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.62 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a 640 GB NVMe SSD. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics are relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 702.0 and a stress-ng single-core CPU performance of 1283.39. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6940.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 24081.11. Memory bandwidth is solid, reaching 1493.1 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 110.4 GB/sec for large-block reads. Database operations perform at an average level, with PassMark database operations scoring 10300.92 and Redis SET operations achieving 10.08 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r3.8xlarge offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require the latest generation of high-frequency single-core processors. The presence of local NVMe SSD storage provides fast local scratch space, though network bandwidth is limited to a baseline of 5 Gbps. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that benefit from its 244.0 GB memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services r3.8xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz. The instance features 244.0 GB of memory, a 640 GB NVMe SSD for local storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 702.0, but average multi-core capabilities, including a PassMark CPU score of 24081.11 and 10.08 million Redis SET operations per second. Memory bandwidth is stable, reaching 110.4 GB/sec for large-block reads. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage, the r3.8xlarge is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, caching systems, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r3.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 244.0 GB of RAM, and a 640 GB NVMe SSD, designed for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r3.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 244.0 GB of RAM, and a 640 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring thirty-two vCPUs, substantial memory capacity, and local NVMe SSD storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
244.0 GB of system memory optimized for memory-bound applications
640 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access
Average multi-core performance suitable for database operations and caching layers
